title: Ardus Kaine
content:

{{Quote|Grand Moff Ardus Kaine is a hard, cold, and extremely calculating man. A brilliant theorist and tactician, he has put great emphasis on success through total domination, an applied principle of the New Order. He views himself as one day taking the position of Emperor, not of the Imperial Galactic Empire, but of the ''New'' Galactic Empire.|New Republic report on Ardus Kaine|<ref name="pent" />}}
'''Ardus Kaine''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] who founded the [[Pentastar Alignment]], and was the successor of [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]] as [[Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] of [[Oversector Outer]] following Tarkin's [[Death/Legends|death]] at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. [[Gender/Legends|His]] [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] was the [[Reaper (Executor-class)|''Reaper'']]. Following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Kaine became a [[Ardus Kaine faction|breakaway]] Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] but soon [[Pentastar Talks|gathered]] local leaders from the [[Velcar Free Commerce Zone]] and established the Pentastar Alignment, an isolationist political conglomerate in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] without interference from the newly-formed [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].

During [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]]'s return from [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], Kaine allied with him and contributed some forces to his [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] but did not officially join him.

==Biography==
===Rise to prominence===
Ardus Kaine was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] native to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Bastion/Legends|Sartinaynian]] in the [[Braxant sector/Legends|Braxant sector]].<ref name="atlas" /> The son of successful [[Merchant/Legends|trader]] and [[Speciesism|speciesist]] [[Vilardo Kaine]], Kaine had all of his father's misplaced ambitions foisted upon him, despite the fact that he was not particularly hateful of non-humans and was given more to co-operation than giving orders. However, Kaine saw his father ascending the highest echelons of [[Politics/Legends|political]] society. Despite not particularly agreeing with his father, he worked hard to please him, eventually becoming involved in politics.<ref name="DOAE1" />

A strong character and noted public speaker, Kaine was involved in the politics and administration of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], becoming one of the earliest, most ardent supporters of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], embracing his philosophy wholeheartedly after his father was killed in a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] attack.<ref name="DOAE1" /> Kaine remained at the Senator's side as Palpatine was elected [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]]; when Palpatine reorganized the Republic into the first [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Kaine adopted the principles of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]] with fanaticism. His leadership and organizational skills came to the fore in the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], during which Kaine played a key role in administrative reform. Immediately after the [[Declaration of a New Order|Declaration of the New Order]], he was instrumental in ensuring the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic's]] support for the new regime. Kaine worked alongside the likes of [[Crueya Vandron/Legends|Crueya Vandron]], [[Ishin-Il-Raz]] and [[Janus Greejatus/Legends|Janus Greejatus]] in the formation of the [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|Commission for the Preservation of the New Order]], after concerns about the administration of [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]], viewing it as something his father would have been proud of.<ref name="DOAE1" /> Kaine quickly became disillusioned with the COMPNOR project, however, viewing the organization's constant feuding with Imperial Intelligence as a waste of valuable Imperial time and effort.<ref name="pent" /> It was around this time he began a lifelong feud with [[Ennix Devian]]: he viewed Devian as a cruel, brutal thug. Devian, in turn saw Kaine as pompous and uncommitted to [[High Human Culture/Legends|Human High Culture]] - something, ironically, that was correct.<ref name="DOAE2">{{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-2|series=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 2|nolive=1}}</ref>

For his loyal and productive Imperial servitude, Kaine was appointed governor of several worlds, and later a [[Moff/Legends|moff]].<ref name="pent" /> Extremely wealthy, Kaine at some point became a corporate backer of the Imperial [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]].<ref name="dfs">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050616a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 6: Outcasts and Megalomaniacs Welcome}}</ref> After [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin's]] [[Death/Legends|death]] at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], he became [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] of [[Oversector Outer]]. While this offered him a prestigious title and control over a huge region of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], Kaine saw his promotion as a personal rebuke: he wanted to be near Palpatine on [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]], assisting in important Imperial policies, not tucked away in the farthest fringes of the Empire trying to repair the damage left by Tarkin's death - even his enemy Devian had managed to be closer to the halls of power.<ref name="DOAE2" /> With Kaine's new title came command of the [[Outer Rim Imperial Fleet]] and the elite [[Scourge Squadron]],<ref name="TEGtW" /> a considerable force of [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] tasked with rooting out and quelling [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] activity in the Outer Rim. Kaine's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] [[Reaper (Executor-class)|''Reaper'']] was one of the few Imperial [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnoughts]] in commission.<ref name="pent" /> Around the time of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], one of the two [[worldcraft]] in orbit around [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] was due to be given to Kaine for his service to the Empire: it was later stolen by Ennix Devian.<ref name="DOAE2" />

After the Battle of Yavin, Grand Moff Kaine was concerned about the situation in [[The Warren (Dantooine)|the Warren]], an Imperial research facility located on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]]. Millions of credits in Imperial funding allocated to the Warren disappeared and its commanding officer, the megalomaniac [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Bors Teraud]], declined to answer to Ardus Kaine's inquiries. Suspecting a treachery, the Grand Moff dispatched an [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Imperial Inquisitor]] and a contingent of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] to inspect the research facility. Indeed, Bors Teraud was a traitor who conducted his own research, creating a [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] army to topple the Emperor. Inquisitor [[Janna bel Arde]] and her troops were allowed to enter the facility but they were soon ambushed and murdered by Teraud's loyalists.<ref name="SWG:TW">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=location|The Warren|Dantooine}}</ref> In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> a group of [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] assaulted the Warren after [[O'Evitt Piboi|one]] of the Imperial workers escaped the facility and reported the horrific experiments conducted there. The spacers collected evidence of Teraud's treachery and eventually delivered it to Imperial authorities.<ref name="SWG:TW" />

===The stars align===
{{Quote|The New Order has never fallen. Only the Emperor. … The Emperor may be dead, but the Empire lives on!|Ardus Kaine, Pentastar Talks|<ref name="pent" />}}
In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Palpatine was [[Death/Legends|killed]] during a Rebel [[Battle of Endor/Legends|attack]] on the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], and the Empire was thrown into a state of disarray. News of the Emperor's demise was slow to reach Oversector Outer, but Kaine was eventually informed aboard the ''Reaper''. In previous times, the loss of his hero would have been met with great sorrow by Kaine, but, still bitter about being stationed in the Outer Rim, he merely saw it as an opportunity. Kaine still believed in the ideals of the New Order, but now the opportunity to found a new Imperial state, with Kaine at its head, had been presented to him. While other Imperials in the Outer Rim initiated chaotic internecine conflicts, Kaine moved quickly to consolidate his power and form a new Imperial faction. Knowing that many corporations would struggle to keep afloat without Imperial backing, Kaine called together a [[Pentastar Talks|gathering]] of corporation heads from the [[Velcar Free Commerce Zone]] and Imperial governors aboard the ''Reaper''.<ref name="pent" />

The "conciliatory meeting" was attended by Imperial [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Ib Dekeet]]; [[Commerce Master Commissioner]] [[Gregor Raquoran]]; [[Dynamic Automata]] Corporate Head [[Elta Besk]]; [[Galentro Heavy Works]] Representative [[Wyrn Otro]]; and Kaine himself. Kaine proposed a treaty which would unite the Imperials and corporate interests to create an isolationist political conglomerate that would practice the ideals of the New Order and allow the allied businesses to continue to trade as under the Empire, without interference from the newly-formed [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Kaine was well aware that, should the Velcar Free Commerce Zone fall under New Republic jurisdiction, local syndicates use of [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] and corruption would be quickly stopped, and he used this fact as leverage during the talks, emphasizing the fact several times. The talks were fraught with paranoia and confrontation, and Kaine had to work hard to keep the participants off each other's throats. As it was, none of Kaine's colleagues particularly cared for his political theory, and Otro openly questioning Kaine's ambitions. The Grand Moff refuted claims that he would install himself as Emperor, emphasizing the need for unity and co-operation to ensure the project was a success. Ultimately, the [[Pentastar Alignment Treaty]] was signed by all parties, creating the [[Pentastar Alignment]] and marking the first official state made independent from the Empire. Kaine dubbed it thus because there were five plenipotentiaries present on his flagship; despite mild objections from Governor Dekeet, all Pentastar vessels and soldiers were adorned with a badge, which Kaine hoped would be a symbol of both unity and fear.<ref name="pent" />

With newfound corporate backing and significant forces of capital ships and soldiers at his disposal, Kaine established the Alignment in a huge number of [[Star system/Legends|systems]], engulfing areas with force and installing planetary overseers to carry out the will of Kaine and his allies. Many Imperial forces in Oversector Outer voluntarily joined the cause, due to Kaine's strong leadership and fervent Imperialism. The Grand Moff kept his burgeoning quasi-Empire mostly independent from the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], refraining from any conflict with the New Republic or rival warlords. As a result, Kaine's business allies were allowed to continue operating free from interference, despite the New Republic's moral opposition to their Humanocentric, oppressive ''modi operandorum''. Ironically, at the same time Kaine was quite happy to use large numbers of non-humans in his [[Pentastar Patrol]] - as his Human High ways were largely a sham, he was pragmatic enough to use them to shore up his military.<ref name="DOAE1" /> Ultimately, aside from dissident pirate groups, the Alignment was most at risk from internal threats. Differences between various Pentastar departments and leaders threatened to destabilize the alliance; though rarely confronted, Kaine was unpopular among many of his colleagues, such as Raquoran.<ref name="pent" />

===Quietus===
The declining Galactic Empire continued to operate on Coruscant, initially under former [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]], until he was ousted by [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] [[Ysanne Isard]], but Kaine paid no heed.<ref name="nec" /> During this period, the Pentastar Alignment helped fund Jerec's search for and expedition to the [[Valley of the Jedi]]; it was ultimately an unsuccessful undertaking, and the former Inquisitor perished in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]].<ref name="dfs" /> The Alignment also became the temporary refuge of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Octavian Grant]], who wished to lay low and avoid the attentions of the New Republic and old Imperial enemies, until he defected to the New Republic in [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]].<ref name="who">{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}}</ref> The Alignment began to come under increased pressure from discordant pirate groups during this period. One such group, the [[Red Moons]], proved a particular thorn in Kaine and the Alignment's side. Led by [[Andrephan Stormcaller]], a former Rebel Alliance and New Republic [[Officer/Legends|officer]] who had resigned his commission after the New Republic refused to intercede against Kaine, the Red Moons made incessant raids of Alignment worlds and [[Assassin/Legends|assassinated]] numerous officials. Kaine's government claimed the Red Moons were operating with tacit New Republic support, though this was refuted.<ref name="bog">{{JournalCite|8|Blaze of Glory (short story)}}</ref>

In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] returned from a sojourn in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] in a last-ditch attempt to reunite and consolidate the faltering Empire.<ref name="nec" /> The Alignment contributed some forces to Thrawn's efforts<ref name="atlas" /> but did not formally join him, preferring to continue its militarization in anticipation of conflict with the New Republic.<ref name="bog" /> [[10 ABY/Legends|One year]] later, the [[Cloning/Legends|resurrected]] Emperor Palpatine began to surreptitiously reunite various Imperial splinter factions, as part of [[Operation Shadow Hand]], an effort to topple the New Republic. The reborn Emperor was more successful than Thrawn in recruiting Kaine's ilk, with the likes of warlords [[Treuten Teradoc]] and [[Blitzer Harrsk]] joining the movement; ultimately, however, it was a failure, with Palpatine defeated for the last time on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]].<ref name="nec" /> Kaine was killed during the [[Battle of Palanhi (Galactic Civil War)|campaign]] over the planet [[Palanhi/Legends|Palanhi]], when his [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] was ambushed and destroyed by several New Republic [[E-7 E-wing multi-role starfighter|E-wing fighters]].<ref name="TEGtW" /> While Kaine believed he was meeting Octavian Grant in the belief he would rejoin the Pentastar Alignment, in reality it was a deception by [[Ennix Devian]] using stolen New Republic fighters.<ref name="DOAE2" />

===Legacy===
The Pentastar Alignment was annexed by [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], becoming part of the [[Imperial Remnant]].<ref name="nec" /> ''Reaper'' became Pellaeon's flagship until its destruction at the [[Battle of Celanon]] in [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]].<ref name="atlas" /> Before [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], Kaine's homeworld Sartinaynian—renamed Bastion—became the Imperial Remnant's capital, remaining thus for over a century.<ref name="Leg">''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
[[File:Kane.jpg|thumb|left|120px|Grand Moff Ardus Kaine]]
Ardus Kaine was noted for his ambition and hunger for power. He cared more for actual power than titles or prestige; the office of Grand Moff did not appeal to him, nor did the title of Emperor of the Pentastar Alignment. He wanted to be at the core of the Empire, making important decisions, being involved in the day-to-day running of the Imperial machine. He was also opportunistic, taking chances as soon as they were presented to him. He moved to establish his Imperial faction post with haste after learning of the Imperial defeat at Endor, and after its founding, organized the power structure so that Kaine was a ''de facto'' autocrat.<ref name="pent" />

As one of Palpatine's earliest converts to the ideals of the New Order, Kaine was fervently loyal to the Empire and everything it stood for. He truly believed in the tenets of the New Order, and believed that enforcing them on subjugated worlds was justified. The core Imperial precepts and mandates were, Kaine felt, somewhat neglected and under enforced, and in his own Empire, they were zealously adhered to and held a more significant position.<ref name="pent" />

What few realized was that much of this was a carefully presented and maintained front - Kaine was largely indifferent to the Human High Culture that many of his contemporaries believed in and did not seek power for the sake of it. Kaine was motivated by the memory of his father, who had foisted upon him all of these ideals, and sought to impress him, even though the elder Kaine was now dead. He wanted to rule not because he genuinely wanted it, but because his father had drilled it into him from his earliest days. However, it also fostered an intense inferiority complex in Kaine: with his father dead, Kaine felt genuine fear at the prospect of power - when he established the Alignment, one of his chief, yet secret reasons for its isolation from the ongoing [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] was that Kaine preferred to rule successfully over a smaller kingdom like the Alignment than lose it all in a fool's gamble for the position of the new [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Emperor]]. The irony of this was that some, like [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Octavian Grant]], genuinely viewed Kaine as having the leadership ability to successfully rule.<ref name="DOAE1" />

The Grand Moff's rhetorical acumen was well known as used by the Empire. His subtle, persuasive oration was convincing; flitting between aggression, tacit intimidation, and syllogism, he was rarely dissuaded. These skills came to use after the Declaration of the New Order, when Kaine helped garner military support for Palpatine's fledgling regime. Later, during the Pentastar Talks, Kaine used this acuity in his role as arbiter to hold together the collapsing negotiations, and subtly influence proceedings in his favor. He was not a man partial to failure, and his determination ensured he often succeeded against odds others would have given up against, as in the case of the talks aboard ''Reaper''. During the tenure of the Pentastar Alignment, Kaine's ability to exhort his colleagues ensured he was rarely opposed on any decision.<ref name="pent" />

<!--Cold, calculating. Moral issues.

Theorist, tactician.

Organizational/administrative skills. Efficiency.

Well-protected.-->
